name: Chicken Breasts Parmesan
recipeIngredient: 1 cup dried bread crumbs (fresh will also do)
2/3 cup Parmesan cheese (fresh grated if possible)
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a meat mallet, flatten each chicken breast into an oval shape.
2. Get out three bowls and put the flour in the first one.
3. Lightly beat the eggs in the second bowl.
4. In the third bowl, combine the bread crumbs with the salt, onion powder, garlic powder, and paprika.
5. Dip the flattened chicken breasts first into the flour, then into the beaten eggs, and finally cover well with the seasoned crumbs.
6. Place each prepared chicken breast on a rack and put them in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to let the coating set.
7. Prepare the Sauce:
a. Make an Italian-seasoned tomato sauce by first lightly sauteing the chopped onion and garlic.
b. Add the two cans of tomato sauce, oregano, and basil. Simmer for 20 minutes.
8. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet and saute the chicken breasts until they are golden brown.
9. Arrange the sauted chicken breasts in a shallow baking dish.
10. Pour the sauce over the chicken breasts, spreading so they are lightly covered with the sauce.
11. Top with the grated Parmesan cheese.
12. Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes.
13. Slice the mozzarella cheese into thin slices.
14. Remove the baking dish from the oven and arrange the sliced mozzarella on top of the chicken.
15. Return to the oven for another 10 minutes to melt the mozzarella and serve.
